free_text: The energy level structure of 154Sm from the
University of Alberta 6 MV van de Graaff.
Gamma-ray spectra were obtained at maximum neutron
energies ranging from 1.1 to 2.3 MeV. At each energy,
three separate runs were made: (i) a run with the
scatterer, (ii) a background run with the scatterer
removed, and (iii) a run with an Fe sample. The Fe
runs served as a check on absolute cross section
scale. A time gate was employed in order to identify
neutrons in the Ge(Li) detector as well as in the
also carried out using the runs without the scatterer.
Summation of gamma production cross sections
originating at the level and subtraction of the gamma
production cross sections feeding the level.
